header.stance: 油價保留通膨尾部風險，實質存續期間漲勢遭遇更強的前端定價

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開盤主論點

7 月 17 日美國公債收盤走勢，由前一交易日溫和的熊市趨平轉為扭轉趨平，凸顯更明顯的政策分化。實質利率回落使長存續期間殖利率下跌，但官員言論與能源市場仍維繫今年稍後升息的可能性，前端因此小幅走高。2s10s 殖利率曲線收窄4個基點。這既不是重返本週稍早的全面通膨降溫漲勢，也不是 7 月 16 日具韌性數據引發的拋售再次上演，而是更具區辨性的收盤：立即行動看似不太可能，但等待的代價仍集中在政策敏感的天期。

走勢組成所傳遞的資訊多於名目變動本身。10年期實質殖利率下跌4個基點，10年期損益兩平通膨率則上升2個基點。偏軟的基礎經濟活動訊號、科技股跌勢加深及溫和的存續期間買盤，壓低實質折現率。與此同時，美國與伊朗再度互相攻擊，推動原油價格急升，使名目債券漲勢無法成為明確的通膨降溫結論。好壞不一的美國數據強化這項分化：進口成本壓力仍相當可觀，但住宅營建激增源於多戶住宅，許可證轉弱，製造業產出則停滯。

聯準會定價延續本週的核心變化。市場隱含 7 月升息機率已降至約 15%，遠低於溫和的消費者與生產者物價數據公布前的水準。然而，克里夫蘭聯準銀行總裁 Beth Hammack 加入更廣泛的鷹派陣營，強調通膨持續、勞動市場接近充分就業，以及企業對能源、供應鏈、保險及 AI 需求的憂慮。因此，本次收盤延續前2份報告所述 Fed 保持耐心的解讀，同時重新打開今年稍後的政策邊界。即使下次會議未採取行動，油價與具韌性的需求仍可能迫使 Fed 回應。

信用與跨資產市場也呈現相同分化。整體上市信用資產變動不大，但超大規模雲端業者債券持續吸收龐大的全球發行量，而 Cotality 僅在價格、結構與契約條款作出重大讓步後才完成再融資。由於投資人質疑 AI 支出的經濟效益，科技股遭到拋售，將股票的存續期間弱勢與壓抑高評級債券投資組合的供給連結起來。歐洲通膨降溫支持 ECB 暫停立即緊縮，日本較強的成長前景與印度對油價的敏感度則使全球政策路徑持續分歧。主導論點是：實質存續期間漲勢受到能源引領的通膨尾部風險與選擇性融資紀律約束，而非金融情勢普遍寬鬆。

## 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美國公債最終以扭轉走勢收盤。2年期殖利率上升2個基點至 4.18%，5年期持平於 4.28%，10年期下跌2個基點至 4.55%，30 年期則下跌3個基點至 5.06%。即使長端從週四實質殖利率轉貴的走勢中反彈，政策敏感型債券仍未跟隨上漲。這項次序符合市場仍認為 Fed 沒有立即行動的充分理由，卻要求對限制性政策維持更久或今年稍後恢復升息的可能性提供保護。

收盤時，2s10s 斜率為 37 個基點，2s30s 為 88 個基點。兩者皆因長端表現優於前端而趨平。2s5s10s 蝶式利差維持-17 個基點，2s10s30s 則下移3個基點至-14。第一組蝶式利差穩定，且10年期相較2年期與 30 年期表現更強，顯示本交易日並非一致性的存續期間買盤，而是集中於中期與長期實質殖利率，2年期則仍與 Fed的反應函數緊密相連。

每週變化讓這波走勢保持適當視角。2年期殖利率較1週前低3個基點，5年期低2個基點，10年期低1個基點；長債則持平。過去1個月，所有列示天期均上升 12 至 13 個基點。溫和的 6 月通膨報告在本週關於下次會議的爭論中勝出，但並未消除自 6 月中旬以來累積的較高實質利率及期限溢價門檻。

通膨連結美國公債區分了名目收盤走勢背後的2股力量。10年期實質殖利率收於 2.31%，10年期損益兩平通膨率為 2.24%。5年期與7年期實質殖利率各下跌3個基點至 2.01% 和 2.15%，20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率則下跌4個基點至 2.67% 和 2.87%。前端以外的實質存續期間需求相當廣泛。油價上漲推高通膨補償，因此10年期名目債券的漲幅小於其實質組成部分的跌幅。

6 月貿易價格使通膨管線維持活躍。進口價格在 5 月增幅修正為 1.7% 後上升 0.3%，年增 7.1%，為 2022 年 8 月以來最大年度漲幅。燃料進口價格在 5 月大漲 12.6% 後下跌 0.4%，但非燃料價格月增 0.4%、年增 4.2%。出口價格則在前月增幅修正為 1.2% 後下跌 0.6%。出口價格的月度反轉，並未抵銷高漲的非燃料進口成本持續向國內傳導的風險。

住宅市場的表面數字強於其利率敏感核心。新屋開工大增 19.0%，達年化 142.7萬戶，主要由多戶住宅營建帶動；獨棟住宅開工則下滑 0.2% 至 895,000 戶。建築許可下降 3.0% 至 136.7萬戶，其中獨棟住宅許可減少 2.4% 至 871,000 戶。完工量上升 3.3%，獨棟住宅完工量增加 6.6%。這項組合指向波動性較高的營建提振與更多完工供給，而非家庭新屋需求明確加速。

工業生產僅增加溫和的成長壓力。6 月產出上升 0.1%，製造業持平，採礦與公用事業則各成長 0.4%。產能利用率維持在 76.1%，較長期平均低 3.3 個百分點。初步消費者信心提供較強的對照，汽油價格下跌改善情緒，使其升至5個月高點。1年期通膨預期由 4.6% 降至 4.2%，但油價再度飆升，意味這項改善所依據的燃料背景截至週五已經改變。

Hammack的言論解釋了2年期券為何未跟隨長存續期間上漲。她在風險排序中將通膨置於勞動市場疲弱之上，形容就業接近她估計的充分就業水準，並提到能源、供應鏈、保險及 AI 各領域的價格壓力。她的警告緊接著達拉斯聯準銀行總裁 Lorie Logan 主張提高利率。主席 Kevin Warsh 先前也已表示，本週較佳的通膨數據並不代表抗通膨任務完成。交易員大致排除 7 月行動，但仍持續定價年底前至少升息1次。

國庫券殖利率曲線維持有序，並在同一時間範圍附近呈現溫和分化。4週、6週與8週期等值殖利率降至 3.71%、3.70% 及 3.72%。13週期殖利率上升1個基點至 3.80%，17 週期維持在 3.85%，26 週期與 52 週期則各上升1個基點至 3.92% 及 4.00%。最短天期走低，顯示沒有立即的現金壓力；較長國庫券走強，則保留對稍後政策升息的補償。

因此，本次利率收盤在未完全解決任何1項訊號的情況下，調和了3項訊號。溫和的 6 月通膨與週五好壞不一的經濟活動數據，降低升息急迫性；鷹派官員與高漲的可貿易財成本，使政策分布維持不對稱；油價衝擊推升預期通膨，避險情緒則壓低實質殖利率。殖利率曲線趨平是這項組合的自然表現：長存續期間獲得避險價值，前端則仍須反映 Fed 政策。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

融資仍可取得，但初級市場條件與既有債務的結果具有高度選擇性。美國公債殖利率下降支撐投資等級債總報酬，LQD 上漲 0.06% 至 $107.56。HYG 下跌 0.19% 至 $79.65，JNK 下跌 0.10% 至 $95.98；相較科技股拋售與油價衝擊，跌幅溫和。整體指標並未顯示普遍清算，但個別借款人在價格、文件與資本結構方面面臨大幅度調整。

超大規模雲端業者債務提供最重要的高評級警訊。Alphabet、Meta、Amazon 及 Oracle 自 2025 年初以來已發行超過 $3,000億，其中近 $800億以英鎊、歐元、日圓及其他非美元貨幣計價。這段期間發行的產業債券中，約 79%的利差較首個交易日擴大。其價格平均較發行價下跌 3.3 點，總報酬為-1.4%，使其躋身各發行市場中表現最弱的債券之列。

這項壓力反映供給集中，甚於市場普遍質疑其近期償債能力。由於美元市場無法獨力吸收融資速度，大型科技發行人迅速進入海外指數。Amazon 最新 $250億交易的超額認購程度，對大型 AI 交易而言異常偏低。保險業買家仍有承接空間，但受基準約束的投資組合隨發債加速而累積曝險。週五晶片股拋售使債券市場問題更為明顯：AI 投資報酬只要令人失望，就可能削弱股票估值，而持續的資本計畫則使信用供給維持龐大。

Cotality 證明，較低品質借款人在將可觀價值轉讓給投資人後，仍能完成大額融資。這家房地產資料公司籌得 $52.5億，在需求冷淡後將部分融資組合由債券轉為貸款。其 $28億定期貸款以 98 定價，利差為 425 個基點。$16.5億第一順位留置權債券以面額定價、殖利率 9%；$8億第二順位留置權債券則以 95 定價、殖利率約 13.3%，為今年發行的美國垃圾債最高殖利率。

交易執行除了借貸成本較高，也提供更強保障，以防止庭外重整手法。Cotality的 S&P 評級較投資等級低6個級距，負債接近盈餘的8倍，3 月底現金約為 $5,500萬。募得資金用於再融資5年前為 $60億收購案承擔的債務。交易完成後，前次收盤尚未解決的配售問題告1段落，但代價是提高債務償付負擔，而且在預期自由現金流非常有限之際，仍須依賴循環信貸額度。

Foundever的債權人協議顯示，當槓桿已無法依原始條件償付時，價值如何重新分配。參與貸款人將以既有債權每一單位換取 57.5 美分的新定期貸款，取得重整後約 35%的股權，並共同分配 $2.5億現金償付款。Mulliez 家族將注入 $2.25億新股權，其餘由公司提供。債務預計減少約 $9億。指導委員會成員另可多收回10美分，使債權人所處位置與表面去槓桿數字同樣關鍵。

槓桿貸款生態體系面臨更廣泛的獲利限制。CLO 市場規模約為 $1.3兆，但底層貸款表現疲弱，已降低股權持有人可取得的剩餘報酬。併購相關貸款供給短缺，加上軟體業債務虧損，壓縮了資產收益與 CLO 融資成本之間的套利空間。這項壓力不一定會立即中斷優先順位融資，但可能使邊際貸款需求對價格更為敏感，並促使管理人轉向現金流特性不同的基礎建設或設備資產。

已公布的融資計畫顯示，借款人仍將公開市場視為可行的再融資與投資管道。Deutsche Bank 正為 Baker Tilly 籌備約 $30億債務，以取代其與 Moss Adams 合併相關的私人信貸貸款。San Antonio 規劃發行 $9.44億市政債，為期 20 年的機場擴建計畫融資，其中包括一座造價約 $17億的新航廈。這些是計畫融資額，並非需求結果。在未償 AI 債券供給已對全球高評級投資組合構成壓力之際，這些計畫將承接考驗由槓桿再融資延伸至市政基礎建設。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

歐元區通膨為已開發市場存續期間帶來最明確的紓解。6 月 HICP 年增率由 5 月的 3.2% 降至 2.8%，符合初值，月減 0.1%。服務業對年度通膨的貢獻仍達 1.51 個百分點，能源為 0.77，食品、酒類及菸草為 0.29，非能源工業品則為 0.18。22 個歐盟國家的通膨下降。儘管年增率仍高於1年前水準，其廣泛程度與月度方向均降低 ECB 立即回應的必要性。

經濟學家預期 ECB 將在 7 月會議維持借貸成本不變，多數人則預計在新預測出爐後，9 月升息一碼，使存款利率升至 2.5%。官員幾乎未發現薪資動態使能源衝擊根深蒂固的跡象，但 Bundesbank 總裁 Joachim Nagel 仍保持警戒立場。因此，利率管道取決於持續性：服務業走軟及燃料傳導受控有利德國公債存續期間；石油與天然氣流動若再度受阻，則可能重新推高通膨補償，並將較高的歐洲期限溢價傳導至美國公債。

日本呈現不同的平衡。BOJ 會議前的報導顯示，官員可能在 6 月升息後將政策利率維持於 1%，並考慮上調截至明年 3 月止財政年度的 0.5% 成長預測。儘管能源進口成本上升，AI 相關出口需求仍支撐獲利與所得。企業加速轉嫁成本，加上通膨預計高於目標，使再次升息仍在考量之中；隔夜交換市場顯示 10 月前採取行動的機率約為 69%。日本成長增強會提高國內殖利率門檻，並可能與海外存續期間需求競爭。

3個月期日本國庫券標售顯示，在這條正常化路徑下，短端承接運作正常。第 1395 期以 0.9618%的加權平均殖利率決標，最低得標價格相當於 0.9695%，並有 30.3121% 於該價位獲配。競價投標總額為 ¥11.5723兆，得標金額為 ¥2.89786兆，另有 ¥9,021億非價格競爭投標獲接受。該證券於 7 月 21 日發行，10 月 19 日到期。結果確立在所列決標水準的需求，但未解決買家將如何承接較長天期 JGB 供給的問題。

因此，歐洲與日本向全球存續期間傳遞方向相反的邊際訊號。即使油價居高，歐元區通膨降溫仍使保持耐心更為容易。日本則結合較強成長、更快成本轉嫁及已經較高的政策利率，使進一步正常化的可能性持續存在。美國公債長端受惠於週五美國實質殖利率漲勢，但海外殖利率曲線仍透過相對殖利率、貨幣避險及國內儲蓄配置形成相互競爭的力量。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

儘管長天期美國公債殖利率下降，新興市場債券仍小幅走弱。EMB 下跌 0.06% 至 $95.53，1週跌幅為 0.48%，1個月跌幅為 1.18%。美元在紐約收盤時近乎持平，因此共同的貨幣衝擊有限。油價造成更重要的分化：出口國獲得潛在收入緩衝，進口國則面臨更高的補貼、通膨及經常帳負擔。基金的小幅下跌掩蓋了各國與資本結構之間的重大差異。

印度仍暴露於這項分化的不利一側。印度央行總裁 Sanjay Malhotra 表示，在通膨近一年半來首次升破央行目標後，通膨仍處高檔。印度近 90%的石油仰賴進口，而歷來近半數進口量經由荷莫茲海峽運輸。印度央行將政策利率維持於 5.25%；盧比穩定在每美元約 96.32，10年期政府債券殖利率約為 6.75%。攻擊再起及季風偏弱，可能壓縮政策忽略輸入成本的空間。

巴西在不放棄美元的情況下，尋求主權融資多元化。政府正考慮最快於今年首次在中國境內發行約人民幣100億元、即 $14.8億的熊貓債。官員希望建立主權基準，為在中國有重大業務的巴西企業開啟人民幣融資管道。外匯連結債務僅占巴西總債務的 4%，而該國先前已在其史上最大規模的全球交易、也是十多年來首次歐元發債中籌得 €50億。多元化能擴大買家基礎，但也增添貨幣及市場准入的考量。

Neuquén 展示地方政府重返外部市場的途徑。這個阿根廷頁岩油產省已獲准發行最高 $5億，並尋求5至7年期、到期1次還本、利率 7% 至 8%的債券，為 2017 年以來首次國際發債。石油產量增加改善其收入前景，阿根廷利差收窄也已使數個省份得以赴海外發債，即使中央政府仍未重返市場。到期1次還本風險與對大宗商品的依賴，使 Neuquén 有別於由廣泛稅收能力支持的主權再融資。

因此，新興市場情勢既非廣泛的美元流動性緊縮，也不是大宗商品帶來的一致性利益。印度的進口能源曝險可使油價直接傳導至當地政策與財政成本。巴西規劃中的熊貓債，考驗新貨幣市場能否補充既有全球發債。Neuquén 則利用頁岩油收入與省級政府的樂觀情緒尋求硬通貨融資。美國長期實質殖利率下降有利外部折現率，但各借款人的油價敏感度、收入基礎、到期結構與投資人群體仍是約束性條件。

## 跨資產

美國股票市場將 AI 辯論轉化為集中的避險走勢，強化實質存續期間買盤。S&P 500 下跌 1.01%，Nasdaq 100 下跌 1.49%，費城半導體指數下跌 1.63%。半導體指數全週下跌 9.97%，較1個月前水準低逾 12%。VIX 上升 12.19% 至 18.77。波動率顯著增加，但走勢仍集中於科技業，並未演變成信用與股票市場不分標的的清算。

中國開發商 Moonshot 推出的低成本模型，加劇市場對 AI 競爭、產能及基礎建設投資回收期的疑問。目前強勁的獲利與需求尚未遭到破壞，但晶片與雲端業者的估值對長期資本支出的容忍度已經降低。同樣的重新定價也透過超大規模雲端業者供給及指數集中度反映在債券市場。因此，科技股下跌透過避險情緒與較低實質折現率支撐美國公債，同時警示債務融資的投資仍可能構成信用技術面的阻力。

能源市場朝相反方向移動。隨著美國與伊朗互相攻擊持續，WTI 上漲 4.48% 至 $82.49，Brent 上漲 4.59% 至 $88.10。兩者單週漲幅分別達 15.52% 與 15.91%。油價走勢解釋了損益兩平通膨率為何在名目美國公債上漲期間攀升，也解釋前端為何未跟隨長存續期間的買盤。這也挑戰消費者通膨預期的改善，因為後者反映的是最近局勢升級前較低的汽油價格。

美元未對任何一方提供太多助力，收盤近乎持平於 100.75，全週仍小幅走低。黃金期貨上漲 0.68% 至 $4,012.70，重返具心理意義的價位，但全週仍下跌 2.23%。儘管戰事升級，黃金反應仍受抑制，顯示較低的實質殖利率與逢低買盤，被能源壓力可能使政策利率維持高檔的預期抵銷。跨資產走勢所描繪的是通膨風險疊加於特定產業的成長與估值衝擊，而非資金湧入所有避險資產的典型避險潮。

週一亞洲早盤出現較晚的發展，並非造成週五固定收益收盤的原因。隨著攻擊範圍擴大至軍事目標以外，Brent 曾上漲 3.8% 至超過 $91，美元兌多數貨幣走強。日本因假期休市，亞洲時段美國公債現貨未交易，直接的利率反應因此延後。即使科技業風險持續支撐實質存續期間，油價漲勢延續仍提高週五損益兩平通膨率升勢在現貨債券恢復交易後持續的機率。

## 市場前瞻意義

7 月 23 日美東時間下午 1:00的 $210億新發10年期 TIPS 標售，以及其後 7 月 24 日上午 10:00 公布的 6 月新屋銷售，將區分通膨保護需求與利率敏感型成長。若標售承接良好、標售後實質殖利率穩定，隨後住宅需求疲弱或低迷，將確認週五的解讀：即使油價支撐損益兩平通膨率，存續期間仍能透過實質利率下降而上漲。若標售後實質殖利率大幅轉貴，尤其同時出現新屋銷售具韌性及原油再次上漲，則供給壓力將與較強需求結合，挑戰這項分化。10年期實質殖利率、損益兩平通膨率、2年至10年期殖利率曲線及房貸敏感型信用資產，是 Fed 於 7 月 28-29 日開會前可觀察的傳導管道。

7 月 23 日的 ECB 決策及 7 月 24 日美東時間凌晨 2:00的英國零售銷售，構成下一項歐洲存續期間考驗。若政策措辭強調 6 月通膨降溫、薪資外溢有限及有等待空間，隨後英國家庭需求疲弱，將確認歐洲的能源曝險主要透過實質經濟活動轉弱發揮作用，並支持德國公債與英國公債相對美國公債的存續期間。若政策更著重第2輪價格效應，或英國消費在借貸成本高漲下仍然堅挺，則將挑戰此觀點並恢復近期緊縮壓力。德國公債與英國公債前端、歐元區損益兩平通膨率、歐元與英鎊，以及美國公債殖利率曲線長端，將顯示歐洲政策分歧是在緩解還是加重全球存續期間負擔。

日本 7 月 24 日公布的全國 CPI，將界定 BOJ 較強的成長評估能否與正常化暫停並存。若通膨維持廣泛且處於高檔，同時確認 AI 相關出口正推升成長前景，將強化市場對下半年較早時點再次升息的預期，並推高 JGB 殖利率與日圓。若價格明顯降溫，或再度出現能源成本削弱家庭需求的證據，則將挑戰提前升息的解讀，並降低近期國內殖利率門檻。JGB 殖利率曲線前端、日圓、長天期 JGB 標售需求，以及日本投資人對海外債券的相對偏好，是傳導至美國公債存續期間的關鍵節點。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 實質殖利率走低之際能源通膨風險再起，公債殖利率曲線扭轉趨平
overall_summary.session_narrative: 美國公債漲勢集中於前端以外：中期與長期實質殖利率下跌，損益兩平通膨率則上升。好壞不一的住宅與工業數據，加上科技股領跌的拋售，增添避險需求；但美國與伊朗再度互相攻擊推高原油價格，使通膨補償維持。整體信用市場並未出現清算，但超大規模雲端業者大量供給，以及讓利豐厚的較低品質融資，使這波走勢並未釋放金融情勢普遍寬鬆的訊號。
overall_summary.summary: 長天期美國公債上漲，政策敏感的前端則小幅走弱，使殖利率曲線在 7 月 17 日紐約收盤時趨平。偏軟的經濟活動訊號與科技業風險壓低實質殖利率，但油價飆升、進口成本堅挺及 Fed 鷹派言論，使今年稍後升息的風險持續存在。

section_summaries.market_read: 本次收盤宜採分化解讀：實質存續期間獲得紓解，但前端政策壓力未減。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價與損益兩平通膨率能否維持，將顯示實質殖利率的漲勢能否延續。
section_summaries.summary: 收盤走勢由全面的單向交易轉為扭轉趨平：長天期實質殖利率下跌，前端則仍受今年稍後政策風險牽制。偏軟的經濟活動與科技業疲弱支撐存續期間，但油價上漲和持續的成本壓力，使市場無法形成明確的通膨降溫訊號。

section_summaries.market_read: 殖利率曲線趨平反映長存續期間的避險需求，而非市場對近期寬鬆抱持信心。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察油價與具韌性的需求使 Fed 路徑維持不對稱時，實質殖利率是否轉為便宜。
section_summaries.section_title: 美國公債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 實質殖利率下跌帶動長天期債券表現領先，2年期殖利率則上升，殖利率曲線斜率趨平。6 月通膨數據降低立即行動的急迫性，但進口成本壓力、鷹派官員及油價上漲，使前端仍面臨稍後政策回應的風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用市場的韌性掩蓋了整體市場平靜與個別交易紀律之間日益擴大的落差。
section_summaries.next_check: 在科技業供給仍然龐大之際，追蹤即將發行的槓桿融資與市政債交易需求。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 融資管道維持暢通，但承接情況取決於借款人品質及更有利於投資人的條件。超大規模雲端業者大量發債拖累全球高評級投資組合；Cotality 則僅透過更高成本、結構性讓利及債權人保障完成再融資，凸顯整體信用市場穩定表象下的選擇性紀律。

section_summaries.market_read: 歐洲與日本的政策預期背道而馳，海外殖利率曲線因而抑制美國存續期間買盤。
section_summaries.next_check: ECB 措辭與日本 CPI 將檢驗全球分歧是會緩和，還是會推高期限溢價。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 歐元區通膨降溫支持 ECB 近期保持耐心，日本較強的成長與持續通膨則使 BOJ 進一步正常化仍在視野之中。相反的政策訊號，使全球存續期間在歐洲帶來的紓解與日本較高的殖利率門檻之間拉扯，並透過相對殖利率、避險及儲蓄資金流動傳導。

section_summaries.market_read: 美國實質殖利率下降有利新興市場債券，但油價敏感度與債務結構仍居主導地位。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察印度的通膨因應，以及投資人對巴西與 Neuquén 發債的接受程度。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場債券小幅走弱，但未發生廣泛的美元衝擊，油價曝險與融資結構仍是主要區隔因素。印度面臨輸入性通膨風險，巴西探索人民幣融資，Neuquén 則尋求以頁岩油收入支持的硬通貨融資管道，但面臨到期1次還本風險。

section_summaries.market_read: 跨資產走勢支持實質存續期間，但也警示能源可能使政策風險維持高檔。
section_summaries.next_check: 觀察油價壓力持續至下一個現貨收盤時，科技業疲弱是否擴大。
section_summaries.summary: 科技股領跌及波動率上升，透過降低實質折現率與增加避險需求支撐美國公債。與此同時，原油飆升推高通膨補償，而美元近乎持平及黃金反應克制，證實這是疊加通膨因素的產業衝擊，而非全面避險潮。

section_summaries.market_read: 存續期間主論點目前取決於供給、成長與政策考驗是否會維持分歧。
section_summaries.next_check: 監測3項預定考驗是否帶來一致的實質殖利率轉貴，或再度引發存續期間需求。
section_summaries.summary: 3項考驗將決定實質殖利率下跌與通膨補償堅挺的分化能否持續：美國的通膨保護需求與住宅、歐洲的政策與消費，以及日本的通膨成長組合。其影響將透過殖利率曲線斜率、損益兩平通膨率、海外前端利率及海外對美國公債的需求傳導。

watch_events.market_implication: 這項組合將挑戰分化式漲勢，並對房貸敏感型信用資產構成壓力。
watch_events.market_trigger: 標售後實質殖利率大幅轉貴，同時新屋銷售具韌性且原油走強。
watch_events.title: 美國 TIPS 需求與住宅考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 先追蹤10年期 TIPS 標售，再觀察 6 月新屋銷售對實質殖利率與殖利率曲線的影響。

watch_events.market_implication: 前端緊縮壓力將回歸，並加重全球存續期間負擔。
watch_events.market_trigger: 政策強調第2輪通膨效應，或英國消費在高借貸成本下仍然堅挺。
watch_events.title: ECB 與英國需求考驗
watch_events.watch_line: 在 ECB 指引與英國零售銷售考驗歐洲耐心之際，追蹤德國公債與英國公債前端。

watch_events.market_implication: BOJ 提前緊縮的預期將推升 JGB 殖利率及美國公債的投資門檻。

watch_events.watch_line: 利用全國 CPI，透過 JGB 前端、日圓與債券需求檢驗 BOJ 暫停升息的判斷。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

The July 17 Treasury close replaced the prior session’s modest bear flattening with a twist flattening that exposed a sharper policy split. Long-duration yields fell as real rates retraced, yet the front end edged higher because officials and energy markets kept later-year tightening alive. The 2s10s curve narrowed 4 basis points. This was neither a return to the broad disinflation rally seen earlier in the week nor a renewal of the July 16 resilience selloff. It was a more discriminating close in which immediate action looked unlikely, but the cost of waiting remained concentrated in policy-sensitive maturities.

The composition carried more information than the nominal move. The 10-year real yield fell 4 basis points while 10-year breakeven inflation rose 2. Softer underlying activity signals, a deepening technology selloff and a modest bid for duration lowered the real discount rate. At the same time, renewed US-Iran attacks drove crude sharply higher, preventing the nominal rally from becoming a clean disinflation verdict. Mixed US releases reinforced the split: import-cost pressure remained substantial, but the housing surge came from multifamily construction, permits weakened and manufacturing output stalled.

Federal Reserve pricing preserved the week’s central change. The implied likelihood of a July increase had fallen to about 15%, far below the level seen before the benign consumer- and producer-price releases. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack nevertheless joined a broader hawkish chorus, emphasizing persistent inflation, a labor market near maximum employment and business concern about energy, supply chains, insurance and AI demand. The close therefore extended the patient-Fed interpretation from the prior 2 reports while reopening its later-year boundary. Oil and resilient demand can still force a response even if the next meeting passes without action.

Credit and cross assets made the same separation visible. Broad listed credit moved little, but hyperscaler bonds continued to absorb a large global issuance burden and Cotality completed its refinancing only after major price, structure and covenant concessions. Technology equities sold off as investors questioned the economics of AI spending, linking equity-duration weakness to the supply weighing on high-grade bond portfolios. European disinflation supported a pause in immediate ECB tightening, whereas Japan’s firmer growth outlook and India’s oil sensitivity kept global policy paths divergent. The governing thesis is a real-duration rally constrained by an energy-led inflation tail and selective funding discipline, not a generalized easing of financial conditions.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasuries ended in a twist. The 2-year yield rose 2 basis points to 4.18%, the 5-year was unchanged at 4.28%, the 10-year fell 2 to 4.55%, and the 30-year declined 3 to 5.06%. Policy-sensitive paper resisted the rally even as the long end recovered from Thursday’s real-yield cheapening. The ordering fits a market that still saw little reason for immediate Fed action but demanded protection against the possibility that restrictive policy lasts longer or resumes tightening later in the year.

The closing slopes were 37 basis points for 2s10s and 88 for 2s30s. Both flattened as the long end outperformed the front end. The 2s5s10s butterfly held at -17 basis points, whereas 2s10s30s moved 3 basis points lower to -14. Stability in the first fly and richer 10-year performance relative to the 2- and 30-year points show that the session was not a uniform duration bid. It concentrated around intermediate and long real yields while the 2-year stayed tied to the Fed’s reaction function.

Weekly changes kept the move in perspective. 2-year yields were 3 basis points lower than a week earlier, 5-year yields were down 2 and 10-year yields were down 1; the long bond was unchanged. Over 1 month, every quoted maturity was 12 to 13 basis points higher. The benign June inflation reports won the weekly argument about the next meeting, but they did not erase the higher real-rate and term-premium hurdle accumulated since mid-June.

Inflation-linked Treasuries separated the two forces behind the nominal close. The 10-year real yield ended at 2.31% and the 10-year breakeven at 2.24%. 5- and 7-year real yields each fell 3 basis points to 2.01% and 2.15%, while 20- and 30-year real yields declined 4 basis points to 2.67% and 2.87%. Real-duration demand was broad beyond the front end. Higher oil raised inflation compensation, leaving the 10-year nominal rally smaller than the fall in its real component.

June trade prices kept the inflation pipeline active. Import prices rose 0.3% after a revised 1.7% May increase and were 7.1% higher over the year, the largest annual rise since August 2022. Fuel import prices fell 0.4% after May’s 12.6% jump, but nonfuel prices increased 0.4% on the month and 4.2% over the year. Export prices fell 0.6% after a revised 1.2% gain. The monthly reversal in exports did not offset the continuing domestic pass-through risk from elevated nonfuel import costs.

Housing looked stronger at the headline than in its rates-sensitive core. Starts jumped 19.0% to a 1.427 million annualized pace, driven by multifamily construction, while single-family starts slipped 0.2% to 895,000. Permits fell 3.0% to 1.367 million, including a 2.4% decline in single-family permits to 871,000. Completions rose 3.3%, with single-family completions up 6.6%. The mix points to a volatile construction boost and additional finished supply, not a decisive acceleration in household demand for new homes.

Industrial production added only modest growth pressure. Output rose 0.1% in June, manufacturing was unchanged, and mining and utilities each gained 0.4%. Capacity utilization held at 76.1%, which was 3.3 percentage points below its long-run average. Preliminary consumer sentiment offered a firmer counterpoint, rising to a 5-month high as lower gasoline prices improved morale. 1-year inflation expectations eased to 4.2% from 4.6%, though the renewed oil surge means that improvement was based on a fuel backdrop that had already changed by Friday.

Hammack’s comments explained why the 2-year sector did not follow long duration higher. She placed inflation above labor-market weakness in her risk ranking, described employment as near her maximum-employment estimate and cited price pressure across energy, supply chains, insurance and AI. Her warning followed Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan’s call for higher rates. Chair Kevin Warsh had already said the week’s better inflation data did not complete the inflation task. Traders largely removed July action but continued to price at least 1 increase by year-end.

The bill curve remained orderly and mildly divided around the same horizon. 4-, 6- and 8-week coupon-equivalent yields fell to 3.71%, 3.70% and 3.72%. 13-week yield rose 1 basis point to 3.80%, the 17-week held at 3.85%, and 26- and 52-week yields each rose 1 basis point to 3.92% and 4.00%. Easing at the shortest maturities argues against immediate cash pressure, while firmer longer bills preserve compensation for a later policy increase.

The rates close consequently reconciled three signals without fully resolving any of them. Soft June inflation and uneven Friday activity reduced the urgency to tighten; hawkish officials and elevated traded-goods costs kept the policy distribution asymmetric; the oil shock lifted expected inflation even as risk aversion lowered real yields. A flatter curve was the natural expression of that combination, with long duration receiving protection value and the front end remaining accountable to the Fed.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Funding remained available, though primary terms and legacy-debt outcomes were highly selective. LQD gained 0.06% to $107.56 as lower Treasury yields supported investment-grade total returns. HYG fell 0.19% to $79.65 and JNK declined 0.10% to $95.98, modest losses beside the technology selloff and oil shock. The broad gauges showed no generalized liquidation. Individual borrowers faced much larger adjustments in price, documentation and capital structure.

Hyperscaler debt supplied the most important high-grade warning. Alphabet, Meta, Amazon and Oracle have issued more than $300 billion since the start of 2025, including almost $80 billion in sterling, euros, yen and other non-dollar currencies. About 79% of sector bonds sold over that period traded at wider spreads than on their 1st day. They were down an average 3.3 points from issue price and returned -1.4% in total, placing them among the weakest performers in each market where they were sold.

The pressure reflects supply concentration more than a broad doubt about near-term solvency. Large technology issuers entered foreign indexes rapidly because the dollar market alone could not absorb the financing pace. Amazon’s latest $25 billion transaction attracted unusually low oversubscription for a jumbo AI deal. Insurance buyers still had room, but benchmark-constrained portfolios accumulated exposure as issuance accelerated. Friday’s chip selloff made the bond-market problem more visible: any disappointment in AI investment returns could weaken equity valuations while a continuing capital program keeps credit supply heavy.

Cotality demonstrated that lower-quality borrowers could still clear size after transferring meaningful value to investors. The property-data company raised $5.25 billion, shifting part of the package from bonds to loans after lukewarm demand. Its $2.8 billion term loan priced at 98 with a spread of 425 basis points. A $1.65 billion first-lien bond priced at par to yield 9%, and an $800 million second-lien bond priced at 95 to yield about 13.3%, the highest yield for a US junk bond issued this year.

Execution came with stronger protection against out-of-court restructuring maneuvers as well as higher borrowing costs. Cotality is rated six notches below investment grade by S&P, carries debt near 8 times earnings and had about $55 million of cash at the end of March. Proceeds refinance obligations used for its $6 billion acquisition 5 years earlier. The completed deal resolved the immediate placement question left open at the prior close, but it did so by raising debt service and preserving reliance on a revolving facility amid very modest expected free cash flow.

Foundever’s creditor agreement showed how value can be redistributed once leverage is no longer serviceable on original terms. Participating lenders are set to exchange 57.5 cents of existing claims for a new term loan, receive roughly 35% of the restructured equity and share a $250 million cash paydown. The Mulliez family will provide $225 million of new equity, and the company will contribute the balance. Debt is expected to fall by about $900 million. Steering-committee members receive an additional 10 cents of recovery, making creditor position as consequential as the headline deleveraging.

The leveraged-loan ecosystem carried a broader profitability constraint. The CLO market totals about $1.3 trillion, yet weak underlying loan performance has reduced the residual return available to equity holders. A shortage of merger-related loan supply and losses in software debt have compressed the arbitrage between asset income and CLO funding costs. That pressure need not interrupt senior financing immediately, but it can make marginal loan demand more price-sensitive and push managers toward infrastructure or equipment assets with different cash-flow profiles.

Announced financing plans showed that borrowers still viewed public markets as a viable refinancing and investment channel. Deutsche Bank was preparing roughly $3 billion of debt for Baker Tilly to replace private-credit loans associated with its merger with Moss Adams. San Antonio outlined a $944 million municipal sale for a 20-year airport expansion, including a new terminal costing about $1.7 billion. These are pipeline amounts, not demand results. They extend the absorption test from leveraged refinancing into municipal infrastructure as outstanding AI supply already weighs on global high-grade portfolios.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Euro-area inflation delivered the clearest developed-market duration relief. Annual HICP slowed to 2.8% in June from 3.2% in May, matching the preliminary reading, and prices fell 0.1% on the month. Services still contributed 1.51 percentage points to annual inflation, compared with 0.77 from energy, 0.29 from food, alcohol and tobacco, and 0.18 from non-energy industrial goods. Inflation declined in 22 EU states. The breadth and monthly direction reduced the need for an immediate ECB response even though the annual rate remained above its year-earlier level.

Economists expected the ECB to hold borrowing costs at its July meeting, with most anticipating a quarter-point increase in September to a 2.5% deposit rate after new forecasts arrive. Officials had found little sign of wage dynamics entrenching the energy shock, but Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel maintained a vigilant stance. The rate channel therefore depends on persistence: softer services and contained fuel transmission support Bund duration, whereas renewed disruption to oil and gas flows could restore inflation compensation and transmit higher European term premium into Treasuries.

Japan presented a different balance. Reporting ahead of the BOJ meeting indicated officials were likely to keep the policy rate at 1% after June’s increase and consider raising the 0.5% growth forecast for the fiscal year ending next March. AI-related export demand had supported profits and income despite higher energy import costs. Faster corporate pass-through and inflation projected above target kept another increase in view, with overnight swaps assigning about a 69% chance of action by October. Stronger Japanese growth raises the domestic yield hurdle and can compete with demand for foreign duration.

The 3-month Japanese bill auction showed functioning short-end absorption within that normalization path. Issue No. 1395 cleared at a 0.9618% weighted-average yield, with the lowest accepted price equivalent to 0.9695% and 30.3121% allotted there. Competitive bids totaled ¥11.5723 trillion against ¥2.89786 trillion accepted, alongside ¥902.10 billion of accepted non-price-competitive bids. The security issues July 21 and matures October 19. The result established demand at the quoted clearing levels without settling how buyers will receive longer JGB supply.

Europe and Japan thus sent opposite marginal signals into global duration. Euro disinflation made patience easier even with oil elevated. Japan combined stronger growth, faster cost pass-through and an already higher policy rate, preserving the prospect of further normalization. The Treasury long end benefited from Friday’s US real-yield rally, but foreign curves still impose competing forces through relative yields, currency hedging and the allocation of domestic savings.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market debt softened modestly despite lower long Treasury yields. EMB fell 0.06% to $95.53, leaving it down 0.48% over 1 week and 1.18% over 1 month. The dollar was nearly unchanged at the New York close, so the common currency shock was limited. Oil created the more important split: exporters gained a potential revenue cushion, while importers faced higher subsidy, inflation and current-account burdens. The small fund decline concealed substantial country and capital-structure differences.

India remained exposed to the adverse side of that divide. RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra described inflation as elevated after it moved above the central bank’s target for the first time in almost a year and a half. India imports nearly 90% of its oil, and almost half of those flows historically moved through the Strait of Hormuz. The RBI held its policy rate at 5.25%; the rupee was steady near 96.32 per dollar and the 10-year government yield near 6.75%. Renewed attacks and a weak monsoon could narrow the room to look through imported costs.

Brazil sought to diversify sovereign funding without abandoning the dollar. The government was considering an inaugural onshore Chinese panda bond of about 10 billion yuan, or $1.48 billion, as soon as this year. Officials want a sovereign benchmark that could open renminbi funding to Brazilian companies with material Chinese operations. Foreign-exchange-linked debt accounts for only 4% of Brazil’s total, and the country had already sold €5 billion in its largest global transaction and 1st euro offering in more than a decade. Diversification broadens the buyer base but adds currency and market-access considerations.

Neuquén illustrated a sub-sovereign route back to external markets. Argentina’s shale-producing province had authorization for as much as $500 million and was seeking a 5- to 7-year bullet bond with a 7% to 8% interest rate, its 1st international issue since 2017. Stronger oil production improves the revenue story, and narrower Argentine spreads have already enabled several provinces to issue abroad even as the national government stays out. Bullet maturity risk and commodity dependence keep Neuquén distinct from a sovereign refinancing backed by broad tax capacity.

The EM picture was therefore neither a broad dollar-liquidity squeeze nor a uniform commodity benefit. India’s imported-energy exposure can transmit oil directly into local policy and fiscal costs. Brazil’s planned panda bond tests whether a new currency market can complement established global issuance. Neuquén is using shale income and provincial optimism to seek hard-currency access. Lower US long real yields helped the external discount rate, yet each borrower’s oil sensitivity, revenue base, maturity structure and investor pool remained the binding terms.

## Cross asset

US equities reinforced the real-duration bid by turning the AI debate into a concentrated risk-off move. The S&P 500 fell 1.01%, the Nasdaq 100 lost 1.49%, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index declined 1.63%. The semiconductor gauge finished the week down 9.97% and more than 12% below its month-earlier level. The VIX rose 12.19% to 18.77. Volatility increased materially, although the move remained centered on technology rather than becoming an indiscriminate liquidation across credit and equities.

A low-cost model from Chinese developer Moonshot intensified questions about AI competition, capacity and the payoff period for infrastructure investment. Strong current earnings and demand had not yet broken, but chip and cloud valuations became less tolerant of long-dated capital spending. The same repricing appeared in bonds through hyperscaler supply and index concentration. Falling technology equities therefore supported Treasuries through risk aversion and a lower real discount rate while simultaneously warning that debt-funded investment can remain a credit technical headwind.

Energy moved in the opposite direction. WTI rose 4.48% to $82.49 and Brent gained 4.59% to $88.10 as US-Iran attacks continued. Their weekly advances reached 15.52% and 15.91%. The oil move explains why breakeven inflation rose during a nominal Treasury rally and why the front end resisted the bid in long duration. It also challenges the improvement in consumer inflation expectations, which reflected lower gasoline prices before the latest escalation.

The dollar offered little reinforcement to either side, closing almost flat at 100.75 and remaining slightly lower over the week. Gold futures gained 0.68% to $4,012.70, recovering the psychologically important level while still losing 2.23% on the week. Bullion’s restrained response despite war escalation suggests that lower real yields and dip buying were offset by expectations that energy pressure could keep policy rates high. Cross assets described inflation risk layered onto a sector-specific growth and valuation shock, not a classic flight into every haven.

Early Monday Asian trading brought a later development that did not cause Friday’s fixed-income close. Brent rose as much as 3.8% to more than $91 as strikes expanded beyond military targets, and the dollar strengthened against most peers. Cash Treasury trading was closed during Asian hours for a Japanese holiday, delaying the direct rates response. The extension of the oil rally increases the chance that Friday’s rise in breakevens becomes persistent when cash bonds reopen, even as technology risk continues to support real duration.

## Forward Market Significance

The $21 billion new 10-year TIPS auction at 1:00 p.m. ET on July 23, followed by June new-home sales at 10:00 a.m. on July 24, will separate inflation protection demand from rates-sensitive growth. A well-absorbed auction with a stable post-sale real yield, followed by weak or subdued home demand, would confirm Friday’s interpretation that duration can rally through lower real rates even as oil supports breakevens. Sharp real-yield cheapening after the auction, especially alongside resilient home sales and another advance in crude, would challenge that separation by joining supply pressure to stronger demand. The 10-year real yield, breakeven inflation, the two-to-10-year curve and mortgage-sensitive credit are the observable channels before the Fed meets July 28-29.

The ECB decision on July 23 and UK retail sales at 2:00 a.m. ET on July 24 form the next European duration test. Policy language that emphasizes June’s disinflation, limited wage spillovers and scope to wait, followed by soft household demand in Britain, would confirm that Europe’s energy exposure is operating mainly through weaker real activity and support Bund and gilt duration relative to Treasuries. A stronger focus on second-round price effects, or firm UK consumption despite elevated borrowing costs, would challenge that view and restore near-term tightening pressure. Bund and gilt front ends, euro-area breakevens, the euro and sterling, and the long end of the Treasury curve would reveal whether European policy divergence is easing or amplifying the global duration burden.

Japan’s national CPI release on July 24 will define whether the BOJ’s stronger growth assessment can coexist with a pause in normalization. Inflation that remains broad and elevated, combined with confirmation that AI-linked exports are lifting the growth outlook, would reinforce expectations of another increase earlier in H2 and raise JGB yields and the yen. A marked easing in prices or renewed evidence that energy costs are weakening household demand would challenge the earlier-hike interpretation and reduce the immediate domestic yield hurdle. The front of the JGB curve, the yen, long JGB auction demand and Japanese investors’ relative appetite for overseas bonds are the key transmission points into Treasury duration.

section_summaries.market_read: The close favors a split reading: real-duration relief without front-end policy relief.
section_summaries.next_check: Persistence in oil and breakevens will show whether the real-yield rally can hold.
section_summaries.summary: The close replaced broad directional trading with a twist flattening: long real yields fell while the front end remained pinned by later-year policy risk. Softer activity and technology weakness supported duration, but higher oil and persistent cost pressures prevented a clean disinflation signal.

section_summaries.market_read: Curve flattening reflects protection demand at duration, not confidence in near-term easing.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether real yields cheapen as oil and resilient demand keep the Fed path asymmetric.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Long maturities outperformed as real yields declined, while the 2-year yield rose and curve slopes flattened. June inflation data reduced urgency for immediate action, yet import-cost pressure, hawkish officials and higher oil left the front end exposed to a later policy response.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit resilience masks a widening gap between broad market calm and deal-level discipline.
section_summaries.next_check: Track demand for upcoming leveraged and municipal deals as technology supply remains heavy.
section_summaries.summary: Funding remained open, but absorption depended on borrower quality and stronger investor terms. Heavy hyperscaler issuance weighed on global high-grade portfolios, while Cotality cleared refinancing only through higher costs, structural concessions and creditor protections, exposing selective discipline beneath stable broad credit.

section_summaries.market_read: Foreign curves temper the US duration bid as Europe and Japan pull policy expectations apart.
section_summaries.next_check: ECB language and Japan CPI will test whether global divergence eases or raises term premium.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Euro-area disinflation supported near-term ECB patience, while Japan's stronger growth and persistent inflation kept further BOJ normalization in view. Opposing policy signals left global duration pulled between European relief and a higher Japanese yield hurdle transmitted through relative yields, hedging and savings flows.

section_summaries.market_read: Lower US real yields help EM debt, but oil sensitivity and debt structure still dominate.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch India's inflation response and investor reception for Brazil and Neuquen issuance.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: Emerging-market debt softened modestly without a broad dollar shock, leaving oil exposure and financing structure as the main separators. India faced imported-inflation risk, Brazil explored renminbi funding, and Neuquen sought hard-currency access backed by shale revenue but exposed to bullet maturity risk.

section_summaries.market_read: Cross assets support real duration while warning that energy can keep policy risk elevated.
section_summaries.next_check: Watch whether technology weakness broadens as oil pressure persists into the next cash close.
section_summaries.summary: Technology-led equity losses and rising volatility supported Treasuries through lower real discount rates and protection demand. At the same time, surging crude lifted inflation compensation, while a nearly flat dollar and restrained gold response confirmed an inflation-layered sector shock rather than a broad flight to safety.

section_summaries.market_read: The duration thesis now depends on whether supply, growth and policy tests preserve divergence.
section_summaries.next_check: Monitor the 3 scheduled tests for coordinated real-yield cheapening or renewed duration demand.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the split between falling real yields and firm inflation compensation can persist: US inflation-protection demand and housing, European policy and consumption, and Japan's inflation-growth mix. Their transmission runs through curve slopes, breakevens, foreign front ends and overseas demand for Treasuries.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

bullets: 10Y TIPS real yield 2.31% (-4 bp) | 10Y breakeven inflation 2.24% (+2 bp)
UST 2s10s curve 37 bp (-4 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 88 bp (-5 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Treasury curve twist-flattened as lower real yields met renewed energy inflation risk
overall_summary.session_narrative: The Treasury rally was concentrated beyond the front end: real yields fell across intermediate and long maturities while breakeven inflation rose. Uneven housing and industrial data, together with a technology-led equity selloff, added protection demand, but renewed US-Iran attacks lifted crude and preserved inflation compensation. Broad credit avoided liquidation, yet heavy hyperscaler supply and concession-rich lower-quality financing kept the move from signaling a general easing in financial conditions.
overall_summary.summary: Long Treasuries rallied while the policy-sensitive front end edged weaker, leaving the curve flatter at the July 17 New York close. Softer activity signals and technology risk lowered real yields, but surging oil, firm import costs and hawkish Fed commentary kept later-year tightening risk alive.

watch_events.market_implication: That combination would challenge the split rally and pressure mortgage-sensitive credit.
watch_events.market_trigger: Sharp post-auction real-yield cheapening with resilient home sales and firmer crude.
watch_events.title: US TIPS demand and housing test
watch_events.watch_line: Follow the 10-year TIPS auction, then June new-home sales, across real yields and the curve.

watch_events.market_implication: Front-end tightening pressure would return and increase the global duration burden.
watch_events.market_trigger: Second-round inflation emphasis or firm UK consumption despite high borrowing costs.
watch_events.title: ECB and UK demand test
watch_events.watch_line: Track Bund and gilt front ends as ECB guidance and UK retail sales test European patience.

watch_events.market_implication: Earlier BOJ tightening expectations would lift JGB yields and the hurdle for Treasuries.
watch_events.market_trigger: Broad, elevated inflation combined with confirmation of stronger AI-linked export growth.
watch_events.title: Japan CPI and BOJ normalization
watch_events.watch_line: Use national CPI to test the BOJ pause through JGB front ends, the yen and bond demand.
